title: ReactionTarget
description: A ReactionTarget addresses a single handler invocation for a single subject. `lib.classify.ClassifyResult.targets: frozenset[ReactionTarget]` carries any number per classified event. The bridge's dispatch loop coalesces by `debounce_key` within a drain pass. The handler-log records `{ts, agent, handler, target_id, debounce_key, payload}` for each dispatched ReactionTarget; consumers observing the log validate against this schema.

handler.description: Name of the registered handler to dispatch to. Handlers live in `lib/handlers.py` and the per-agent classifier module. Conventional shape: lowercase, underscore-separated (e.g., `log_intent`, `notify_channel_mcp`).

target_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject being addressed. Often duplicates the source Intent's `subject_id` but may differ when one Intent fans out to multiple targets (e.g., a cross-board move addresses both source and dest boards as separate targets).

debounce_key.description: Coalescing key. Within a single drain pass, multiple ReactionTargets sharing the same `debounce_key` collapse into one handler invocation. Across drain passes, the `DebounceTracker` (`lib/debounce.py`) re-suppresses if the same key was dispatched within the configured window. Conventional shape: `<resource>:<id>:<action>` (e.g., `task:42:any`).

debounce_seconds.description: Per-target debounce-window override. `null` uses the consumer's default (30s out of the box). Present-but-null is the conventional 'inherit default' marker.

payload.description: Handler-specific structured data. Schema is per-`handler`. JSON object on the wire; in-memory shape is a frozen tuple-of-tuples (use `ReactionTarget.payload_dict()` for the object form).
